subject: Project Management
title: Denver International Airport Baggage
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Denver International Airport Baggage.

hook.text: Denver's automated baggage system was supposed to revolutionize airports. It shredded luggage instead.

buildup.text: The system used 26 miles of track, 5,000 telecars, and 14,000 motors — all designed before testing began.

discovery.text: When turned on, bags were misrouted, damaged, and flung off conveyor belts. The system was a mechanical nightmare.

twist.text: The airport opened 16 months late, costing $560 million extra. The automated system was eventually ripped out entirely.

climax.text: A system too complex for waterfall: too many variables, too many edge cases, too little incremental testing.

punchline.text: They designed the whole system at once. It failed all at once too.

quiz.question: What went wrong with Denver's automated baggage system?
options: It was fully designed before testing, and was too complex to work as planned
It was too simple for the airport
